architecture_summary: The paper proposes UNetFormer, a UNet-like Transformer for efficient semantic segmentation of remotely sensed urban scene imagery. It uses a lightweight ResNet18 encoder and a Transformer-based decoder built from global-local Transformer blocks (GLTB) with an efficient global-local attention mechanism and a feature refinement head (FRH).

novel_contributions: a UNet-like Transformer (UNetFormer) for efficient semantic segmentation of remotely sensed urban scene imagery
a Transformer-based decoder with a global-local Transformer block (GLTB) that captures both global and local contexts
an efficient global-local attention mechanism with an attentional global branch and a convolutional local branch
a cross-shaped window context interaction module to capture cross-window relations with high computational efficiency
a feature refinement head (FRH) to shrink the semantic gap between shallow and deep features
a fully Transformer structure (FT-UNetFormer) that also achieves state-of-the-art results on the Vaihingen dataset

key_claims.claim: UNetFormer is a UNet-like Transformer that uses a lightweight ResNet18 encoder and a Transformer-based decoder for efficient semantic segmentation of remotely sensed urban scene imagery.
UNetFormer achieved 67.8% mIoU on UAVid and 52.4% mIoU on LoveDA, while reaching up to 322.4 FPS with a 512 × 512 input on a single NVIDIA GTX 3090 GPU.
The proposed Transformer-based decoder combined with a Swin Transformer encoder also achieves the state-of-the-art result on the Vaihingen dataset.

The efficient global-local attention achieves the highest mIoU among the compared attention mechanisms on UAVid and is more accurate and faster than shifted window attention and efficient multi-head self-attention.
UNetFormer outperforms the compared networks on UAVid test set with 67.8% mIoU and strong speed/efficiency trade-offs.
UNetFormer delivers the best F1, OA and mIoU on the Vaihingen test set and achieves 92.8% mean F1 and 86.8% mIoU on Potsdam.
key_claims.evidence_section: 4.3.3 Results on the Vaihingen and Potsdam dataset

key_claims.claim: UNetFormer obtains the highest mIoU on LoveDA with the least complexity and the fastest speed among the compared methods.
The paper argues that the hybrid structure with a CNN-based encoder and Transformer-based decoder is superior for real-time urban scene segmentation.
